description: VDR batch processing tiers for selective scan optimization. Defines which document batches should be processed in which order, with estimated scan times and priority levels.

core_tech.label: Core Tech
core_tech.description: Security assessments, compliance certs, architecture docs — highest signal density. These documents are most likely to contain deal-material findings and should always be processed first.

supporting_context.label: Supporting Context
supporting_context.description: Org charts, financials, product docs — provides context for tech findings. These help practitioners validate and contextualize core tech signals, but are secondary to security/architecture.

uncategorised.label: Uncategorised
uncategorised.description: Documents that don't match known batch rules — may contain surprises. These are lowest priority because they may be marketing materials, historical artifacts, or other non-diligence documents.

notes.core_tech_rationale: Security, compliance, and architectural findings are the primary value drivers in tech diligence. A company with solid security controls, clear architecture, and clean compliance story can proceed to deeper diligence quickly. Core Tech scans establish the risk baseline.
notes.supporting_context_rationale: These batches provide essential context (org structure, financials, product clarity) but are secondary to core tech assessment. Only worth full extraction if core tech signals are borderline or require validation.
notes.uncategorised_rationale: These documents are likely non-diligence materials or low-signal content. They should only be processed if time permits or if specific signals require additional evidence.
notes.timing_note: Estimated times assume Claude API extraction with 500-token average output per document. Actual wall-clock time depends on API latency and document complexity.
notes.practitioner_guidance: For time-constrained diligence, run Tier 1 (Core Tech) immediately. This takes ~25 mins and establishes the risk profile. Flag Tier 2 for practitioner review to decide whether to proceed with full extraction.
